questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a way to set environment variables in a Docker Compose file?
options: Using the 'environment' key with inline values
Using the 'env_file' key to load from a file
Using the 'env_vars' key with a list of values
questions.answer: Using the 'env_vars' key with a list of values

questions.question: What file does Docker Compose automatically read for variable substitution?
In the syntax '${DB_HOST:-localhost}', what does ':-localhost' mean?
options: Use 'localhost' as the default if DB_HOST is not set
Raise an error if DB_HOST is not localhost
questions.answer: Use 'localhost' as the default if DB_HOST is not set

questions.question: How do you specify an alternate .env file when running docker compose?
In a Compose file, what does the 'env_file' key do?
options: Loads environment variables from the specified file into the container
Sets the name of the .env file for variable substitution
Exports container environment variables to a file
questions.answer: Loads environment variables from the specified file into the container

questions.question: What is the purpose of a health check in Docker Compose?
options: To monitor CPU usage of the container
To determine if a container's application is running correctly
To restart Docker daemon when it crashes
To check network connectivity between the host and Docker Hub
questions.answer: To determine if a container's application is running correctly

questions.question: Which health check parameter specifies how often Docker runs the health check command?
What does the 'start_period' parameter in a health check control?
options: How long to wait before starting the container
The grace period during which health check failures are not counted
The delay between container start and the first log entry
How long the container takes to initialize its network
questions.answer: The grace period during which health check failures are not counted

questions.question: What does the 'retries' parameter in a health check specify?
options: How many times to restart the container on failure
How many consecutive failures before the container is marked unhealthy
How many health check commands to run in parallel
How many times to retry pulling the image
questions.answer: How many consecutive failures before the container is marked unhealthy

questions.question: What health check command is used to verify a PostgreSQL container is ready?
What does 'condition: service_healthy' in a depends_on block do?
options: Ensures the dependent service is removed if the dependency becomes unhealthy
Waits until the dependency passes its health check before starting the dependent service
Monitors the dependency and sends alerts if it becomes unhealthy
Restarts the dependency if it becomes unhealthy
questions.answer: Waits until the dependency passes its health check before starting the dependent service

questions.question: What are Docker Compose profiles used for?
options: Grouping services so you can selectively start subsets of services
Creating different Docker networks for isolation
Defining CPU and memory profiles for containers
questions.answer: Grouping services so you can selectively start subsets of services

questions.question: How do you start services that belong to the 'debug' profile?
What happens to a service with a profile assigned when you run 'docker compose up' without specifying any profile?
options: It starts normally like any other service
It starts but in a limited mode

questions.question: A service without any profile assigned will behave in which way?
options: It will never start unless a profile is specified
It always starts regardless of which profiles are activated
It only starts if all profiles are activated
It is treated as a debug-only service
questions.answer: It always starts regardless of which profiles are activated

questions.question: How do you activate multiple profiles at once?
What is the default restart policy for a Docker Compose service?
What does the 'unless-stopped' restart policy do?
options: Restarts the container only on non-zero exit codes
Never restarts the container under any circumstances
Restarts the container always except when it was manually stopped
Restarts the container only during Docker daemon startup
questions.answer: Restarts the container always except when it was manually stopped

questions.question: Which restart policy restarts a container only when it exits with a non-zero exit code?
Under which Compose key do you set resource limits such as memory and CPU?
In the Compose file, how is a memory limit of 256 megabytes specified under deploy resources?
How is a CPU limit of half a core specified in a Compose file under deploy resources?
What does 'docker compose kill api' do?
options: Deletes the api service from the Compose file
Sends a SIGKILL signal to forcefully stop the api container
Removes the api container and its volumes
Stops the api container gracefully with SIGTERM
questions.answer: Sends a SIGKILL signal to forcefully stop the api container

questions.question: What does the 'timeout' parameter in a health check specify?
options: How long to wait before the first health check
The maximum time a single health check command can take before being considered failed
How long to wait between retries
The total time before the container is marked unhealthy
questions.answer: The maximum time a single health check command can take before being considered failed

questions.question: What is the correct format for a health check test using CMD-SHELL in a Compose file?
